Name: curvature-aware_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: A Deterministic Framework for Training-Free Adaptive Optimization
Short Hypothesis: Leveraging curvature-aware diagnostics and deterministic optimization proxies can enable training-free precision routing that guarantees numerical stability and computational efficiency.
Related Work: Existing precision routing methods often rely on heuristic approaches or training-intensive techniques, lacking formal guarantees on numerical stability and computational overhead. This proposal distinguishes itself by introducing a deterministic, curvature-aware framework that operates entirely in a training-free manner, ensuring strict computational bounds and explicit numerical stability analysis.
Abstract: We propose a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework that leverages deterministic optimization proxies and training-free diagnostics to adaptively select precision levels in neural networks. Unlike existing methods that rely on heuristic or training-intensive approaches, our framework guarantees numerical stability and computational efficiency through formalized stability radii, PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, and explicit condition number constraints. The framework employs spectral quantization theory and low-overhead implementation to bridge foundational optimization theory with deployment constraints. Empirical validation demonstrates that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance in precision routing while maintaining strict computational overhead bounds and deterministic execution pathways.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Evaluate the curvature-aware routing framework on benchmark datasets (e.g., CIFAR-10, ImageNet) using a variety of neural network architectures (e.g., ResNet, VGG). Metrics: Accuracy, computational overhead, numerical stability.
Experiment 2: Compare the deterministic optimization proxies with heuristic precision routing methods. Metrics: Convergence speed, generalization performance, computational efficiency.
Experiment 3: Analyze the impact of curvature-aware diagnostics on precision selection. Metrics: Hessian conditioning, gradient covariance decay, feature entanglement.
Experiment 4: Validate the training-free nature of the framework by comparing it with training-intensive precision routing methods. Metrics: Training time, inference time, accuracy.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The deterministic nature of the framework may limit its adaptability to highly dynamic optimization landscapes.
The reliance on curvature-aware diagnostics may introduce computational overhead in certain scenarios.
The framework may not generalize well to extremely large-scale datasets or architectures due to computational constraints.
